code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate support/resistance levels using rolling min/max
    support = df['low'].rolling(window=params['window']).min()
    resistance = df['high'].rolling(window=params['window']).max()
    
    # Calculate RSI for confirmation
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=params['rsi_window']).rsi()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when price bounces off support with RSI oversold
    signals[(df['close'] <= support * (1 + params['threshold'])) & (rsi < params['rsi_low'])] = 1
    
    # Short when price rejects resistance with RSI overbought
    signals[(df['close'] >= resistance * (1 - params['threshold'])) & (rsi > params['rsi_high'])] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/USD exhibits predictable bounce/rejection behavior at dynamically calculated support/resistance levels when confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
